full_text_rationale: Full text describes FailureMem storing historical repair trajectories in a hierarchical failure memory bank and retrieving them as reusable guidance for multimodal automated program repair on SWE-bench Multimodal.
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 010; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Ruize Ma, Yilei Jiang, Shilin Zhang, Zheng Ma, Yi Feng, Vincent Ng, Zhi Wang, Xiangyu Yue, Chuanyi Li, Lewei Lu
titulo: FailureMem: A Failure-Aware Multimodal Framework for Autonomous Software Repair
formato_armazenamento: Failure Memory Bank built from failed multimodal repair trajectories. Each memory entry has contextual, cognitive, and code layers covering issue summary, visual analysis, diagnosis, negative constraint, golden principle, failed patch summary, and golden patch summary.
framework_agente: FailureMem hybrid workflow-agent architecture with active perception tools and a Failure Memory Bank for multimodal automated program repair
metodo_avaliacao: benchmark; ablation; component analysis; retrieval-depth sensitivity
benchmarks_utilizados: SWE-bench Multimodal with 617 GitHub issues across 17 JavaScript repositories; development split used to build the memory bank
metricas_desempenho: resolved rate, pass@1 averaged over three runs, solved instance counts, top-k memory sensitivity, component ablation
metricas_cl: Memory-bank construction from 84 failed development trajectories; contextual/cognitive/code layer ablations; retrieval top-k sensitivity from k=1 to k=10
baseline_comparado: GUIRepair, Agentless Lite, SWE-agent, Zencoder, Computer-Use Agents, commercial systems, and FailureMem ablations
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: 84 structured memory entries built from failed trajectories after excluding 18 successful memory-free repairs from 102 SWE-bench Multimodal development issues; default retrieval k=3.
custo_por_tarefa: USD 0.33 per issue for FailureMem versus USD 0.29 for GUIRepair, reported as about 13% higher inference cost.
resultado_principal: FailureMem improves resolved rate over GUIRepair on SWE-bench Multimodal, with the abstract reporting a 3.7 point gain. The full GPT-5.1 configuration reaches 33.1% versus 29.4% for GUIRepair.
desempenho_vs_baseline: FailureMem reaches 31.1%, 33.1%, 33.3%, 32.5%, and 33.8% resolved rates with GPT-4.1, GPT-5.1, GPT-5.2, Claude 4, and Claude 4.5 respectively, compared with GUIRepair at 28.8%, 29.4%, 29.8%, 28.6%, and 31.5%.
custo_vs_baseline: FailureMem reports higher inference cost than GUIRepair, USD 0.33 versus USD 0.29 per issue, while adding active perception and memory guidance.
limitacoes: Detailed memory contexts increase inference cost; performance depends on diversity and coverage of the offline failure-memory bank; rare or unprecedented failures may lack similar retrieved memories; automated repair still requires regression checks and human oversight.
ameacas_validade: Results depend on SWE-bench Multimodal, model choice, memory-bank construction from development failures, retrieval depth, visual-grounding tools, and GPU resources; larger or different GUI repositories may change memory usefulness.
